source_jobs.title: Business Intelligence Analyst
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BLOCK LABS

Block Labs is a premier technology studio operating at the bleeding edge of Web3, Artificial Intelligence, and iGaming. We don't just ship features; we engineer high-scale, production-grade platforms that power the next generation of digital products.



We are a collective of senior engineers, product strategists, and builders who refuse to compromise on architecture. Whether we are designing autonomous multi-agent AI systems, building decentralized financial infrastructure, or architecting high-frequency iGaming platforms, our standard is excellence.



We move fast, but we build for the long term. If you are looking to work alongside a team that values deep technical expertise, thoughtful system design, and product ownership, Block Labs is where you belong.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for a data-obsessed BI Analyst to turn blockchain and product data into crisp insights that drive growth, retention, and risk controls across our crypto-enabled iGaming products. You’ll own the end-to-end analytics stack for wallet funnels, wagering behavior, lifecycle marketing, and compliance—using SQL, DBT, Python, Amplitude and Tableau as your primary tools. In the future we planning transition from Tableau to other BI tools alternatives like Metabase or Apache Superset or others.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Drive the business forward through proactive analytics - don't just fulfill dashboard requests; spot anomalies, frame the right question yourself, and bring actionable recommendations to Product / CRM / Risk before they have to ask..

 - Build, maintain, and iterate dashboards surfacing KPIs (GGR/NGR, ARPU, LTV, churn, deposit→wager conversion, fraud loss rate).

 - Write production-grade SQL (CTEs, window functions, incremental models) to model clean datasets from Clickhouse and on-chain tables.

 - Build and maintain AI-agent workflows that automate recurring analytics - anomaly investigation, root-cause loops etc.

 - Use Python (pandas, seaborn, sklearn, scipy) for analysis, data quality checks, lightweight ETL/backfills, API pulls (e.g., blockchain providers), and automation.

 - Work with on-chain data: parse EVM logs, token transfers (ERC-20/721), join labeled address datasets, analyze exchange/bridge flows, gas/fee dynamics, and wallet clustering.

 - Partner with Product, Marketing/CRM, Risk/Compliance, and Engineering to define event schemas and tracking plans; ensure data quality, lineage, and documentation.

 - Design and analyze experiments, cohort analyses, and attribution within iGaming and regional compliance constraints.

 - Investigate anomalies (bonus abuse, botting, arbitrage, suspicious wallet rings) and recommend mitigations.

 - Automate recurring reporting and alerting for KPI movements.
   
   
   
   
   MUST-HAVES

 - 2+ years in BI or Data Analytics; crypto or iGaming exposure prefered.

 - Ambitions about building great analytics infrastructure.

 - Good SQL: complex joins, window functions, query optimization, and dimensional modeling.

 - Solid AI-collaboration craft — prompt & context engineering, spec-driven task framing, and eval-driven iteration. Comfortable decomposing business questions into precise, agent-executable steps and designing the right tools for the agent to call.

 - BI Versatility: Expert knowledge of Tableau (LODs, parameters, performance tuning) combined with a strong interest or experience in modern, open-source BI tools like Metabase or Apache Superset.

 - Proficient Python for analytics & data engineering: pandas, modular scripts/notebooks, API integrations, scheduling small jobs, and writing maintainable code.

 - Solid statistics and probability fundamentals for experimentation - hypothesis testing, sample size and power, confidence intervals, and the discipline to distinguish real signal from noise.

 - Practical understanding of crypto wallets and transaction flows.
   
   


NICE-TO-HAVES

 - dbt, Great Expectations/Monte Carlo for data quality; orche

source_jobs.title: Senior Backend Engineer (Go)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BLOCK LABS

Block Labs is a premier technology studio operating at the bleeding edge of Web3, Artificial Intelligence, and iGaming. We don't just ship features; we engineer high-scale, production-grade platforms that power the next generation of digital products.



We are a collective of senior engineers, product strategists, and builders who refuse to compromise on architecture. Whether we are designing autonomous multi-agent AI systems, building decentralized financial infrastructure, or architecting high-frequency iGaming platforms, our standard is excellence.



We move fast, but we build for the long term. If you are looking to work alongside a team that values deep technical expertise, thoughtful system design, and product ownership, Block Labs is where you belong.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are building the backend that powers how players experience our platform: a set of multi-tenant services that turn high-volume player activity into engaging, personalised, and trustworthy experiences. It is a broad and evolving remit, with real room to shape new products and capabilities as the platform grows.

You’ll be joining a senior, highly technical team building greenfield backend services that react to player activity in real time and at scale. We value autonomy, craftsmanship, and long-term thinking, and we’re building event-driven systems designed for resilience, low latency, and trust.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Design and build distributed backend services that power player engagement across a multi-tenant platform.

 - Develop core platform features and services across a broad and evolving set of player-facing domains, from engagement and personalisation to platform integrity.

 - Consume and process high-volume player activity streams, integrating with internal platforms (payments, game providers, player data) and relevant third-party tools.

 - Collaborate with product, frontend, data, and infrastructure teams to deliver reliable, end-to-end systems.

 - Contribute to architectural design and drive technical decisions across services.

 - Ensure scalability, fault-tolerance, and data integrity, including the correctness guarantees required when balances and player-facing decisions are involved.

 - Maintain clean, testable, and well-documented Go code following industry best practices.




ABOUT YOU:

 - 6+ years of experience in backend development, ideally on high-throughput, real-time, or consumer-facing systems.

 - Strong proficiency in Go as a primary backend language; experience with gRPC and RESTful APIs is highly valued.

 - Solid understanding of distributed systems, multi-tenant architectures, and microservices.

 - Experience building consumer-facing or platform services where engagement, personalisation, or platform integrity matter is a strong advantage.

 - Comfortable working in cloud-native environments.

 - Deep commitment to software reliability, observability, and security best practices.

 - Excellent communication skills and a collaborative mindset.




NICE TO HAVE:

 - Experience with rules engines, real-time scoring, or decisioning systems.

 - Background in fintech, real-time gaming and entertainment platforms, or other high-traffic consumer products.

 - Familiarity with event-driven systems, Kafka, or message queues.

 - Prior experience designing APIs for multi-tenant or white-label products.

What kind of culture can I expect?
Mature, mission-driven, and low-ego. We value clarity over noise, outcomes over theatrics, and pace without chaos. If you’re one of the smartest minds in your craft and want to build with other experts, you’ll feel at home here.

source_jobs.title: Data Platform Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BLOCK LABS

Block Labs is a premier technology studio operating at the bleeding edge of Web3, Artificial Intelligence, and iGaming. We don't just ship features; we engineer high-scale, production-grade platforms that power the next generation of digital products.



We are a collective of senior engineers, product strategists, and builders who refuse to compromise on architecture. Whether we are designing autonomous multi-agent AI systems, building decentralized financial infrastructure, or architecting high-frequency iGaming platforms, our standard is excellence.



We move fast, but we build for the long term. If you are looking to work alongside a team that values deep technical expertise, thoughtful system design, and product ownership, Block Labs is where you belong.




THE ROLE

Data & Intelligence now sits at the centre of several products we are developing, and we need a platform that is both dependable and capable of supporting more advanced intelligence over time.

This role reflects that shift. We are designing a new data platform that will act as the backbone for everything from real time decisioning to predictive modelling. As a Data Platform Engineer in the Data Team, you will own the end-to-end real-time pipeline, serving data across a unified analytical warehouse and feature-serving layer. You are not building dashboards. You are engineering the commercial nervous system of a multi-tenant platform designed to scale from one operator to 10x with marginal infrastructure cost.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Design, build, and maintain scalable data pipelines using AWS Glue (PySpark), or equivalent orchestration and transformation tools.

 - Engineer and optimise the ClickHouse warehouse for sub-second query performance across all back-offices.

 - Implement data contracts between back-office and the platform. Onboarding a new operator is a config change, not new tables, topics, or feature views.

 - Build the feature-serving layer providing pre-computed features to AI agents at millisecond latency.

 - Integrate with third-party databases, back-office APIs, and external systems (CRM, affiliates, acquisition platforms).

 - Establish monitoring, alerting, and maintenance procedures including pipeline health checks, freshness monitoring, anomaly detection, and data contract SLA enforcement.

 - Own CI/CD and infrastructure-as-code for data workloads.

 - Collaborate with data scientists, agent engineers, BI developers, and infrastructure teams to translate data requirements into reliable, production-grade pipelines.
   
   


ABOUT YOU:

 - 3+ years building and operating production data pipelines at scale, with hands-on experience across both streaming and batch paradigms.

 - Expertise in Apache Kafka (or Amazon MSK): topic design, consumer group management, offset handling, schema registry operations, and production troubleshooting of lag, rebalancing, and throughput issues.

 - Strong SQL and warehouse engineering skills: experience with columnar analytical databases (ClickHouse strongly preferred, or similar: Druid, BigQuery, Redshift).

 - PySpark / Spark Streaming proficiency: writing transformation jobs that normalise, enrich, and enforce business rules on event streams. Experience with AWS Glue, Apache Airflow, or Apache NiFi is a strong plus.

 - Data modelling discipline: ability to design normalised, multi-tenant schemas where tenant isolation is a filter, not a fork. Experience with data contracts and schema governance.

 - CI/CD and infrastructure-as-code experience: automated testing of data pipelines, version-controlled deployments (CloudFormation, Terraform, or CDK), and familiarity with containerised workloads (ECS Fargate or Kubernetes).

 - Data quality and observability mindset: experience implementing pipeline health monitoring, automated data validation (Great Expectations or equivalent), freshness checks, and anomaly detection.
   
   


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience in iGaming, online ca

source_jobs.title: Technical Product Manager - AI Agent Systems
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BLOCK LABS

Block Labs is a premier technology studio operating at the bleeding edge of Web3, Artificial Intelligence, and iGaming. We don't just ship features; we engineer high-scale, production-grade platforms that power the next generation of digital products.



We are a collective of senior engineers, product strategists, and builders who refuse to compromise on architecture. Whether we are designing autonomous multi-agent AI systems, building decentralized financial infrastructure, or architecting high-frequency iGaming platforms, our standard is excellence.



We move fast, but we build for the long term. If you are looking to work alongside a team that values deep technical expertise, thoughtful system design, and product ownership, Block Labs is where you belong.




THE ROLE

We are hiring a Product Manager to lead the Agent Execution Layer within our iGaming white-label ecosystem. In this role, you define how our agent intelligence "acts" and exactly what information it has access to.

You are responsible for two core pillars: owning the product design, rollout, and orchestration of the Signal Layer and Agent Systems. You will design and ship agents that are both User-Facing (e.g., resolving player issues in real-time) and Internal/Operational (e.g., optimizing back-office execution and company-wide processes).

The Agent PM acts as the orchestrator of autonomous action. You will gather requirements and use cases PMs who own our core modules (Casino, CRM, Cashier, etc.) and work directly with developers to turn complex data and workflows into autonomous actions. Crucially, you are the safeguard of the system architecture, ensuring that Signals, decision engines, and agents remain cleanly separated and reusable across all products.




THE AGENT & SIGNAL SYSTEM

You will manage the system that turns raw casino and player data into automated business outcomes:

 - The Signal Layer: You define the "senses" of the platform. You are responsible for the logic and semantics of the unified intelligence layer—determining which data points from disparate sources must be synthesized into actionable signals.

 - The Agent Execution Layer: You define the roadmap and the decision-logic for the "doers." You bridge the gap between intelligence and action, designing the workflows for autonomous agents that solve high-friction player problems and streamline operator tasks.




WHAT YOU’LL OWN

 - Agent Roadmap & Orchestration: Translating requirements from Module PMs into a clear development roadmap for our autonomous workforce.

 - Full-Trace Observability: Defining the requirements for monitoring and tracing agent decisions. You ensure we have visibility into why an agent made a specific choice at any given node.

 - Semantic Strategy & Governance: Design the logic and "meaning" for a shared Signal Library. You define exactly what data matters, how it is calculated, and ensure these signals are governed and documented within a centralised Feature Catalog.

 - Autonomous Workflows: Designing and managing the lifecycle of autonomous workflows—from Automated CS Resolution to Withdrawal Review Agents and Fraud Review Agents.

 - Operational Performance: Establishing the bar for agent accuracy, resolution rates, and ROI. You are responsible for the material performance and "business intelligence" of the autonomous workforce.
   
   


WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING

 - Map Decision Paths: Work alongside other PMs to define the logical requirements an agent needs to resolve an issue, escalate it, or trigger a secondary process.

 - Gather Signal Requirements: Collaborate with product teams to identify high-value use cases. You will rigorously collect and document definitions, required latency, and source systems for all data signals.

 - Manage the AI Backlog: Prioritize features and agentic capabilities based on business ROI, technical readiness and company’s objectives.

 - Define Success Criteria: Establish clear, measurable KPIs for agen

notes: High-tech roles, emphasis on AI and Web3, fast-paced environment
